items.mistake: Applying the same random augmentation to validation and test sets
items.fix: Use deterministic preprocessing on held-out data; keep random augmentation for training only.
items.why: Random val augmentation injects noise into metrics and hides true generalization gap.
items.example: Random flips on val set make accuracy swing ±5% between runs — use center crop only for val.

items.mistake: Using horizontal flip for tasks where mirror changes the label
items.fix: Audit each transform against label semantics; disable or restrict transforms that flip meaning.
items.why: Augmentation must be label-preserving; wrong invariances teach incorrect decision rules.
items.example: Left/right traffic sign classification: horizontal flip swaps classes — disable flip.

items.fix: Split first; run SMOTE only on the training partition; evaluate on untouched real test rows.
items.why: Synthetic neighbors leak information from test rows into training if SMOTE sees the full dataset.
items.example: SMOTE on all 50k rows then split — test AUC inflated; correct: SMOTE fit on train 40k only.

items.fix: When train and validation error are both high, increase capacity or features first; then add augmentation if val gap appears.
items.why: Augmentation reduces variance, not high bias from too-simple models.
items.example: Linear model on XOR: flips and noise do not help — need nonlinear features.
